english: paid on-demand errand and same-city delivery services

etymology.description: 跑(跑) (pǎo, "run") + 腿(腿) (tuǐ, "leg") suggests the action of using one's legs to run errands or deliver items locally.
etymology.interpretation: 跑(跑) (pǎo, "run") denotes movement, particularly at speed, while 腿(腿) (tuǐ, "leg") emphasizes the physical act of using one's legs. The concept extends to performing errands as a service, using speed and mobility to handle various tasks or deliveries within a city.

examples.chinese: 我晚上没空(我晚上沒空)，所以找人跑腿(所以找人跑腿)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ wǎnshang méi kòng，suǒyǐ zhǎo rén pǎotuǐ.
examples.english: I am not free in the evening, so I hired someone for errands.

examples.chinese: 她在网上提供跑腿服务(她在網上提供跑腿服務)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài wǎngshàng tígōng pǎotuǐ fúwù.

examples.chinese: 当我生病时(當我生病時)，朋友帮我跑腿买药(朋友幫我跑腿買藥)。
examples.pinyin: Dāng wǒ shēngbìng shí，péngyǒu bāng wǒ pǎotuǐ mǎi yào.
examples.english: When I was sick, a friend helped run errands to buy medicine.
